component.name: Ants Terminal
component.summary: Modern, themeable terminal emulator with GPU rendering and plugins

description.p: Ants Terminal is a fast, feature-rich terminal emulator written from scratch in C++20 with Qt6. It targets the capabilities modern terminal users expect — true-color themes, ligatures, hyperlinks, inline images, and a scriptable plugin system — without dragging in a web runtime.
Key features:

ul.li: Eleven built-in themes with full ANSI palette overrides, plus automatic dark/light switching that follows the system color scheme.
Optional OpenGL glyph-atlas renderer for GPU-accelerated draws; QPainter path with ligature-aware text shaping (HarfBuzz) remains the default.
Inline images via Sixel, Kitty graphics protocol, and iTerm2 OSC 1337. Image paste auto-saves to disk — optimized for LLM / coding-agent workflows.
Shell integration via OSC 133 (prompt jump, exit-status marks). OSC 8 hyperlinks, OSC 52 clipboard, OSC 9/777 desktop notifications, OSC 9;4 progress reporting.
Kitty keyboard protocol with progressive enhancement, synchronized output, SGR mouse reporting, focus reporting.
Embedded Lua 5.4 plugin system (sandboxed) with documented ants.* API. Session persistence saves scrollback across restarts.
Built-in Project Audit panel: clazy + cppcheck + semgrep + regex rules with SARIF v2.1.0 export, AI-assisted triage, and confidence scoring.
Configurable keybindings, command snippets, auto-profile rules, SSH bookmarks, and an OpenAI-compatible AI assistant dialog.

screenshot.caption: Main terminal — custom title bar, tab gradient, status bar
Review Changes dialog — per-file diff and apply flow
Project Audit panel — clazy, cppcheck, and grep findings with SARIF export

description.p: A session's worth of user-facing UI fixes plus a new Debug Mode that made them possible. Tools → Debug Mode gives 14 independent category toggles (Paint, Events, Input, PTY, VT, Render, Plugins, Network, Config, Audit, Claude, Signals, Shell, Session) that write timestamped lines to ~/.local/share/ants-terminal/debug.log, enabled narrowly (one category) or broadly (all). The instrument pinpointed a ~54 Hz full-window repaint cascade that had been driving the menubar / dropdown / paste-dialog flicker across several releases.

ul.li: Added — Debug Mode: env-var bootstrap (ANTS_DEBUG=all or ANTS_DEBUG=paint,events,vt) or in-app Tools → Debug Mode menu with checkable categories + Open Log File + Clear Log actions. Hot-path cost when disabled is a single bit-test.

Added — OSC 133 prompt-on-new-line guard: when a shell emits OSC 133 A (prompt start) and the cursor is not at column 0, the terminal nudges it to a new line first. Fixes "prompt glued onto the end of the previous command's output" (e.g. cat file.json with no trailing newline). Equivalent to zsh's PROMPT_SP, but terminal-side so it works for bash / fish / any shell that emits OSC 133.

Fixed — menubar hover flash: QMenuBar now sets autoFillBackground(true) + Qt::WA_StyledBackground so the stylesheet background-color paints reliably under a WA_TranslucentBackground parent; :hover mirrors :selected to close the one-frame pre-selected hover gap; setNativeMenuBar(false) made explicit.

Fixed — paste-confirmation dialog: rewrote to the fully async Review-Changes pattern (heap QDialog + WA_DeleteOnClose + show()/raise()/activateWindow(), no exec(), no QEventLoop). Three prior attempts via QMessageBox::exec, QDialog::exec, and show()+QEventLoop all hit the ApplicationModal-vs-frameless-parent click-swallow regression that was originally diagnosed for the Review-Changes dialog at 0.6.29.

Fixed — Command Palette idle timer churn: the palette's hidden QListWidget ran its QAbstractItemView timer machinery at ~50 Hz from MainWindow construction onward, cascading a full-window repaint every frame. Lazy-create the list in CommandPalette::show() so the view doesn't exist until the user first presses Ctrl+Shift+P.
Fixed — QMainWindow dock animator: setAnimated(false). Ants has no dock widgets, but QMainWindow's built-in QWidgetAnimator ran continuously at idle.

Fixed — Qt UI effect animations disabled globally (UI_AnimateMenu, UI_FadeMenu, UI_AnimateCombo, UI_AnimateTooltip, UI_FadeTooltip, UI_AnimateToolBox). These drove QPropertyAnimation(geometry) at 60 Hz on menu open/close; we have no use case for animated chrome.

Fixed — titlebar button tooltips removed. A Qt / KWin / Wayland quirk fired the QTipLabel show/hide cycle on every frame while the cursor was near a titlebar button, animating the tooltip's geometry at ~60 Hz. Diagnosed via the debug-log system. Button glyphs (✥ / ✕ / ⬜ / ⟩) + hover-colour feedback give enough affordance without tooltip text.

Changed — TerminalWidget base class: QOpenGLWidget → plain QWidget. The GL widget composites its FBO through the parent's backing store and that always invalidates the top-level; PartialUpdate, WA_OpaquePaintEvent, swapInterval(0), and alpha-buffer opt-out all failed to break it. The QPainter path is unaffected (still ligature-aware via QTextLayout / HarfBuzz). The optional GL glyph-atlas renderer is dormant in 0.7.4 and returns later as an embedded QOpenGLWindow via createWindowContainer.
Tests — menubar_hover_stylesheet, paste_dialog_custom, and new terminal_partial_update_mode pin the invariants above (35/35 pass).

description.p: H6.1 of the 📦 distribution-adoption plan lands: Lua plugins inside the Flatpak sandbox. The 0.7.2 manifest shipped with plugin support disabled because org.kde.Sdk//6.7 does not carry lua54-devel and flathub/shared-modules has no Lua 5.4 entry today. 0.7.3 closes that gap with an in-manifest Lua archive module, so a Flatpak build loads plugins the same way the openSUSE, Arch, and Debian builds do.

ul.li: Added — Lua 5.4 archive module in packaging/flatpak/org.ants.Terminal.yml: a dedicated lua module appears before ants-terminal, built from the upstream Lua 5.4.7 source tarball with a pinned sha256 and the linux-noreadline target (Ants links liblua.a statically — pulling readline into the sandbox would be pure bloat). MYCFLAGS="-fPIC" keeps the archive PIE-safe for linking into the ants-terminal executable; installed to /app via make install INSTALL_TOP=/app, which is exactly where CMake's FindLua searches by default when CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/app.

Added — auto-refresh of the Lua tarball hash: the lua module carries an x-checker-data: stanza pointing at the upstream Lua FTP listing with version-pattern: lua-(5\.4\.\d+)\.tar\.gz and a url-template that substitutes the matched version into the canonical tarball URL. Flathub CI opens a PR against the Flathub repo with a refreshed url + sha256 on each Lua 5.4.x point release — no manual hash churn, and 5.5.x majors are excluded (they would break the in-source find_package(Lua 5.4) floor).

Added — feature test tests/features/flatpak_lua_module/: source-grep regression pinning six invariants against the manifest YAML — Lua module precedes ants-terminal, type: archive with a pinned sha256:, MYCFLAGS="-fPIC" in the build commands, make install INSTALL_TOP=/app, the linux-noreadline target (guarding against the default make linux alias to linux-readline), and the x-checker-data stanza with the 5.4.x version-pattern.

Changed — packaging/flatpak/README.md: the "Lua plugins — limitation" section is replaced with a "Lua plugins" section documenting the enabled path (archive-module build, linux-noreadline rationale, x-checker-data automation, success criterion of PluginManager loading a plugin inside the sandbox). Manifest header comment updated to describe the in-manifest module and the automated hash-refresh path.
Changed — ROADMAP.md H6.1 flipped from 📋 to ✅ (shipped in 0.7.3).

description.p: H6 of the 📦 distribution-adoption plan lands: Flatpak packaging. One artifact that runs on every distro that ships flatpak, with a PTY path wired for the sandbox so the user's shell reaches the host's $PATH and filesystem. Unblocks the "packaged everywhere" roadmap gate without per-distro maintenance.

ul.li: Added — Flatpak manifest (packaging/flatpak/org.ants.Terminal.yml): targets org.kde.Platform//6.7 so the KDE SDK brings cmake, ninja, and every Qt6 component the build needs. finish-args cover Wayland + fallback-X11 + DRI, network (AI endpoint + SSH), portals (org.freedesktop.portal.* for global shortcuts / file dialogs), desktop notifications, and XDG config/data directories. Single cmake-ninja module; Ants source enters via type: dir / path: ../.. for local builds and re-points to type: git / url / tag for Flathub submission.

Added — Flatpak host-shell wiring in ptyhandler.cpp: the forked child probes FLATPAK_ID and /.flatpak-info (either signal triggers the branch) and exec's the user's shell via flatpak-spawn --host with --env= forwarding for TERM / COLORTERM / TERM_PROGRAM / TERM_PROGRAM_VERSION (pulled from the ANTS_VERSION compile definition, not a literal) / COLORFGBG, plus --directory=<workDir> gated on !workDir.isEmpty(). The terminal emulator itself (VT parser, renderer, plugin VM) stays inside the sandbox; only the child shell escapes so tools and $HOME are reachable. Same PTY model as Ghostty's Flathub build. Outside Flatpak the existing execlp path runs byte-for-byte unchanged.

Added — packaging/flatpak/README.md: local build (flatpak-builder --install --user), host-shell rationale, Flathub submission workflow, and the documented Lua-plugins-disabled limitation for the initial manifest (plugin support returns once a shared-modules Lua entry is wired up).

Added — packaging/README.md Flatpak section: layout tree adds flatpak/; "Flatpak — org.ants.Terminal.yml" section mirrors the openSUSE / Arch / Debian sections; shared-concerns header updated from three recipes to four.

Added — feature test tests/features/flatpak_host_shell/: source-grep regression test pinning the branch shape — detection probes both signals in an OR, --host and -- separators appear in argv, every TERM var crosses via --env=, workDir gates on isEmpty(), direct-exec fallback is preserved verbatim, _exit(127) is the post-exec fall-through on execvp failure.
Changed — ROADMAP.md H6 flipped from 📋 to ✅ manifest (0.7.2) + 📋 Flathub submission residual.

description.p: First of the 0.8.0 🎨 multiplexing roadmap items lands ahead of the larger mux-server / remote-control work: SSH ControlMaster auto-multiplexing from the bookmark dialog. A second tab to the same host now reuses the first tab's session, skipping the full auth handshake — same precedent as kitty's ssh kitten, Warp's SSH blocks, and iTerm2's SSH profiles.

ul.li: SSH ControlMaster auto-multiplexing: connects opened from the SSH Manager carry -o ControlMaster=auto, -o ControlPath=$HOME/.ssh/cm-%r@%h:%p, and -o ControlPersist=10m when the new ssh_control_master config key is true (default). Opt-out available for sites that forbid lingering multiplex sockets by policy. $HOME resolves in-process via QDir::homePath() so the ControlPath works under dash / POSIX sh; %r@%h:%p are OpenSSH ControlPath substitution tokens and survive shell quoting intact. Feature test ssh_control_master pins six invariants covering token presence/absence, $HOME resolution, positional ordering, and coexistence with the legacy -p / -i / extraArgs flags.

description.p: Shell integration + triggers — the 0.7.0 minor release rolls up the 0.6.1 → 0.6.45 patch-release arc into the theme the 0.7 ROADMAP plotted out. Command blocks become first-class UI, .cast recording ships, semantic history turns path:line:col into Ctrl-click jumps, OSC 133 markers gain HMAC verification, the full iTerm2-parity trigger set lands, the VT parser goes SIMD with a dedicated PTY/parse worker thread, Quake mode runs Wayland-native via layer-shell + GlobalShortcuts portal, and the H1–H4 distribution slice (SECURITY.md, AppStream metainfo, man page, shell completions) takes Ants from "side project" to "distro-ready." No breaking API changes — every plugin that loaded against 0.6.45 loads against 0.7.0.

ul.li: Shell integration: command blocks as first-class UI with OSC 133 prompt/command/output grouping, Ctrl+Shift+Up / Down jumps, collapsible output with "… N lines hidden" summaries, a 2px pass/fail stripe, and per-block right-click menu (Copy Command, Copy Output, Re-run, Fold/Unfold, Share as .cast). Asciinema .cast v2 recording — full-session via Ctrl+Shift+R or per-block via the context menu.

Semantic history: Ctrl-click on a path:line:col capture in scrollback opens the file at the cited position. CWD resolution via /proc/<pid>/cwd so relative paths Just Work. Editor support covers VS Code family, vi-family, nano, Sublime, Helix, Micro, and JetBrains.

Security: shell-side HMAC verification for OSC 133 markers — when $ANTS_OSC133_KEY is set, forged markers are dropped and a status-bar counter surfaces the count. Plugin capability audit UI renders every declared permission as a revocable checkbox in Settings → Plugins. Image-bomb defenses cap total decoded image bytes at 256 MB per terminal across Sixel / Kitty / iTerm2 OSC 1337.

Triggers + plugin events: full iTerm2-parity trigger rule set (bell, inject, run_script, notify, sound, command, highlight_line, highlight_text, make_hyperlink) with an instant flag. OSC 1337 SetUserVar with user_var_changed event. Command-palette plugin registration via ants.palette.register(). New events: command_finished, pane_focused, theme_changed, window_config_reloaded, user_var_changed, palette_action.

Performance: SIMD VT-parser scan — ground-state hot path scans 16 bytes at a time via SSE2 (x86_64) / NEON (ARM64). Dedicated PTY read + VT parse worker thread decouples input from the GUI with bounded back-pressure (≤ 8 batches in flight). Incremental reflow on resize skips the allocation-heavy rewrap round-trip for standalone lines.

Platform — Wayland native: layer-shell Quake mode promotes the window to zwlr_layer_surface_v1 at LayerTop when layer-shell-qt6-devel is present (XCB path preserved for X11). Freedesktop GlobalShortcuts portal wires the toggle-quake id on KDE Plasma 6 and xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland / -wlr; in-app QShortcut fallback stays active on GNOME Shell.

Distribution readiness (H1–H4): SECURITY.md + CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (coordinated-disclosure policy, Contributor Covenant 2.1) clear the Debian / Fedora / Ubuntu security-team review gate. AppStream metainfo + polished desktop entry with Desktop Actions for NewWindow + QuakeMode. groff -man man page at packaging/linux/ants-terminal.1. Shell completions for bash / zsh / fish installed to conventional vendor paths — auto-discovered on system-wide installs. CI runs appstreamcli validate --explain, desktop-file-validate, and groff -man -Tutf8 -wall on every push.

Dev experience: Project Audit gains four new rules (unbounded_callback_payloads, qnetworkreply_no_abort, silent_catch, missing_build_flags, no_ci), fixture-per-check self-consistency enforcement (audit_fixture_coverage), and a build-asan CI lane running ctest + binary smoke under ASan/UBSan on every push. CONTRIBUTING.md covers build modes, test layout, adding an audit rule, version-bump checklist. actions/checkout bumped v4.2.2 → v5.0.1 to pre-empt the 2026-06-02 Node 20 deprecation.

Notes: 0.7.0 is a minor-release rollup of 30 patch releases; the individual 0.6.x CHANGELOG entries remain authoritative for the SHAs they shipped under. Deferred items (EGL swap-with-damage on GL, Domain abstraction, persistent workspaces, Kitty Unicode-placeholder graphics) are explicitly re-scoped to 0.8.0+.

description.p: Deferred VT-standards items from the 10th-audit research report, plus atomic-write hardening for long-lived audit state. The 11th periodic audit returned 0 of ~103 actionable findings — the baseline stays clean. User-visible win: modern Go / Rust TUIs (delta, lazygit, fzf, jj, neovim, bat, most termenv-based tools) can now auto-detect Ants' dark-vs-light theme without the unreliable COLORFGBG env var, and tmux / neovim / kitty-kitten can save-and-restore terminal state around their own output.

ul.li: Added: OSC 10 / 11 / 12 colour queries. Apps that probe \e]10;?\e\\ etc. get the xterm 16-bit-per-channel reply \e]<n>;rgb:RRRR/GGGG/BBBB\e\\. Only the query form is honoured — the set form is deliberately silently dropped so default fg / bg / cursor colour stays theme-driven and in-terminal programs can't inject a theme.

Added: DECRQSS (Request Status String). DCS $q requests for DECSTBM (scroll-region, r), SGR (current attributes, m), and DECSCUSR (cursor shape, q) reply with DCS 1 $ r … per the xterm spec. Unknown settings reply "invalid" (DCS 0 $ r …) rather than dropping silently so callers fall back to defaults. The DECRQSS branch gates on the $q prefix before Sixel's q search, so Sixel image traffic still routes to the image handler.

Changed: atomic JSON writes via QSaveFile for four long-lived audit-state files — audit_rule_quality.json (frequent per-fire / per-suppression writes; corruption loses every rule score), .audit_cache/trend.json (cumulative severity totals), .audit_cache/baseline.json (fingerprints anchoring the "new findings only" filter), and the .audit_suppress v1 → v2 migration rewrite. Torn writes from crash or kill -9 can no longer corrupt any of these. The one-line .audit_suppress append path stays non-atomic on purpose — the loader already skips malformed lines.

Tests: new osc_color_query feature test locks the OSC 10/11/12 response shape plus the query-only gate (set form MUST NOT produce a response); new decrqss test locks the DECSTBM / SGR / DECSCUSR replies, the "invalid" reply bytes, and a Sixel-routing regression guard so the new DECRQSS branch can't eat image traffic.

description.p: Project Audit dialog self-review pass. The 10th periodic audit returned 0 of 55 actionable findings — the nature of the 55 pointed at three bits of friction the dialog was creating for itself: it was auditing its own output, every "is this noise?" verdict needed an individual click, and users had no summary-level readout for "how much of this run was signal?". All three are closed in this release.

ul.li: Added: signal-ratio readout in the summary banner. renderResults() now prints an "X% actionable" figure — signal / (signal + noise) where noise = suppressions + AI-verdict FALSE_POSITIVE — below the severity pills with colour coding (green ≥60%, amber 20–59%, red <20%). A 30-day rolling average pulled from RuleQualityTracker sits next to it as a peer comparison ("30d avg X% actionable, n=Y fires").

Added: batch AI triage. A 🧠 Triage visible (N) button next to the confidence-sort toggle sends every visible, not-yet-triaged finding to the configured LLM in a single JSON request (hard cap 20 per batch; above that it slices and dispatches sequentially). Responses are spliced back by dedup key so a reordering model can't misalign results. Prompt-injection hardening inherited from the single-finding path. Hidden entirely when AI isn't configured.

Changed: isGeneratedFile() now auto-skips prior audit artifacts — docs/AUTOMATED_AUDIT_REPORT_*.{json,md,html,txt} (SHA-256 dedup keys that gitleaks flagged as high-entropy secrets), .audit_cache/ and audit_rule_quality.json (the dialog's own baseline + self-learning ledger; auditing them is circular), and tests/audit_fixtures/** (bad.* fixtures intentionally embed strings third-party scanners misread as real findings). No project configuration needed.

Notes: no schema changes. audit_rule_quality.json and audit_rules.json are read-compatible with prior versions. The 30-day actionable% uses fires as the denominator rather than run count, because run count isn't directly recorded — the label makes the denominator explicit (n=<count> fires).

description.p: Two user-reported regressions closed, plus a quality-of-life default flip. The 0.6.42 focus-redirect guards didn't cure the menubar hover flash — the real culprit was a stylesheet gap, not focus churn. And users coming back to the app expected their tabs to be waiting for them; session persistence now ships default-on, matching every modern terminal (iTerm2, Kitty, WezTerm, Konsole).

ul.li: Fixed: menubar hover no longer flashes. The 0.6.42 pass narrowed focus-redirect firing but didn't address the root cause. The stylesheet defined QMenuBar::item:selected without a matching base QMenuBar::item rule, so Qt fell back to the native style for the non-selected state and composited native drawing under the :selected overlay. Added an explicit transparent-background QMenuBar::item base rule plus a :pressed rule so every menubar item state is owned entirely by the stylesheet; the 0.6.42 focus guards remain as defense-in-depth.

Fixed: tabs are now remembered between sessions by default. Config::sessionPersistence() defaulted to false, so users who never toggled Settings → General → "Save and restore sessions" lost their tab set on every quit. Flipped the default to true; users with session_persistence: false explicit in config.json still get their opt-out honoured — only the absent-key path reads the new fallback. The 5-second uptime floor in saveAllSessions() still protects against crash-on-launch wiping real state.
Changed: Config::sessionPersistence() default flipped from false to true. First-run users now get tab restore out of the box; explicit values in config.json are respected unchanged.

Tests: new tests/features/session_persistence_default/ three-invariant feature test pins the truth table — missing key → true, explicit true → true, explicit false → false. Linked against src/config.cpp only (no GUI dependency). Brings the feature-test count to 25 and the total ctest count to 26.

Internal: cleaned up a -Wshadow warning at mainwindow.cpp:3921 (Review-Changes finalizer lambda's const Theme &th shadowed the outer th; renamed to lth) and dropped an unused <cstdlib> include from the new test.

description.p: Focus-redirect guard rails. Two user-visible regressions from the 0.6.26 auto-return-to-terminal behaviour — a flashing highlight as the mouse crossed the menubar, and the paste-confirmation dialog's "Paste" button silently swallowing mouse clicks — both traced back to the same QApplication::focusChanged handler firing through narrow windows it shouldn't. New guards short-circuit the redirect when a popup is open, when the cursor is over a menu or menubar, or when any top-level QDialog is visible — the last covers the QMessageBox::exec() modality-handshake tick during which activeModalWidget() can briefly be null.

ul.li: Fixed: menubar File / Edit / View hover no longer flickers. The focus-redirect lambda now early-returns when QApplication::activePopupWidget() is non-null and when QApplication::widgetAt(QCursor::pos()) has a QMenu or QMenuBar ancestor. Both guards are mirrored in the deferred QTimer::singleShot(0,…) fire-time block so a menu opened between queue and fire is still honoured.

Fixed: the paste-confirmation QMessageBox no longer swallows clicks on the "Paste" button (pre-0.6.42 only the &Paste mnemonic worked). The redirect now walks QApplication::topLevelWidgets() at queue time and early-returns on any visible QDialog, mirroring the fire-time check and covering the brief show() handshake window where activeModalWidget() returns null.

Tests: new tests/features/focus_redirect_menu_guard/ source-grep pins six invariants on the focusChanged lambda in src/mainwindow.cpp — the popup and menu-hover guards (queue + fire time), the visible-dialog walk, and the five pre-existing guards (activeModalWidget, QAbstractButton, mouseButtons, QDialog parent chain, CommandPalette) that must not regress. Uses the balanced-brace scanner from 0.6.41 to avoid std::regex stack blow-out on a 4000+ line file.

description.p: Quake-mode + OSC 133 UX polish. Surfaces the out-of-focus portal binding state in Settings so users can tell at a glance whether their Quake hotkey escapes focus, and adds a scrollbar-adjacent tick-mark gutter that exposes OSC 133 command boundaries across the full scrollback — jump targets for Ctrl+Shift+Up/Down that were previously invisible.

ul.li: Added: command-mark gutter (show_command_marks, default on) — a 4-px vertical strip just left of the scrollbar draws one tick per PromptRegion, color-coded by last exit status: green (success), red (non-zero exit), gray (in-progress, OSC 133 D not yet emitted). Positions compress linearly so a 50,000-line scrollback stays fully visible in the widget height.

Added: Settings → Terminal → Scrollback toggle "Show command markers in scrollbar gutter" (default on) with a tooltip explaining the color states. Propagates to all live terminals via applyConfigToTerminal — no restart needed.

Added: portal-binding status label in Settings → Quake. One line under the hotkey field reports either "Portal binding active" (green, hotkey works when Ants is unfocused — KDE Plasma 6, Hyprland, wlroots) or "Portal unavailable" (amber, hotkey works only while focused), based on GlobalShortcutsPortal::isAvailable() at dialog construction.

Invariant: gutter drawing gates on m_showCommandMarks AND non-empty promptRegions(), so users without shell integration see nothing change; ticks never overlap the scrollbar thumb (offset by m_scrollBar->width()).

description.p: OSC 133 shell-integration polish. Adds two keyboard-driven "last completed command" actions that complement the existing block-at-cursor right-click menu with no-selection-needed top-level equivalents — the iTerm2 ⇧⌘O / WezTerm CopyLastOutput convention, surfaced through the View menu, the command palette, and configurable keybindings.

ul.li: Added: TerminalWidget::copyLastCommandOutput() slot — walks promptRegions() backwards for the most recent region with commandEndMs > 0 && hasOutput and copies the full output via outputTextAt(idx) (unbounded). Skips in-progress tails so Ctrl+Alt+O during sleep 30 copies the previous command's output, never the partial in-progress one.
Added: TerminalWidget::rerunLastCommand() slot — same backwards-walk / skip-in-progress rule, then writes the command text + \r to the PTY via rerunCommandAt(idx).

Added: View menu entries "Copy Last Command Output" (default Ctrl+Alt+O, config key copy_last_output) and "Re-run Last Command" (default Ctrl+Alt+R, config key rerun_last_command). Both surface automatically in the command palette; Ctrl+Alt+* avoids the already-taken Ctrl+Shift+O (split pane) and Ctrl+Shift+R (record session).

Added: status-bar toasts for discoverability — success path shows "Copied N chars of last command output"; failure path shows "No completed command to copy (enable shell integration)", a load-bearing hint that OSC 133 needs bash/zsh/fish shell-integration hooks.

Invariant: locked by tests/features/osc133_last_command (CTest labels features;fast, part of the release gate) — pins the backwards-walk, the outputTextAt-not-lastCommandOutput choice, the keybinding wiring, and the toast contract.

description.p: Wayland-native Quake-mode, part 2 of 2 — completes the 0.7.0 roadmap item. When the xdg-desktop-portal service is available on the session bus, Ants Terminal now registers its Quake toggle with the Freedesktop Portal org.freedesktop.portal.GlobalShortcuts API (KDE Plasma 6, xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland, xdg-desktop-portal-wlr). The hotkey fires whether or not Ants has focus. On GNOME Shell (portal service present but no GlobalShortcuts backend yet) and on minimal desktops without a portal, the in-app QShortcut fallback from 0.6.38 stays as the activation path — no regression.

ul.li: Added: GlobalShortcutsPortal client class wrapping the three-step D-Bus handshake (CreateSession → session handle → BindShortcuts → Activated) behind a single Qt signal. Handles request-path prediction, custom meta-type registration for the a(sa{sv}) shortcuts array, and session lifecycle.
Added: portal-aware Quake setup in MainWindow — when the portal is available, binds the toggle-quake id with config.quake_hotkey as the preferred trigger.
Added: focused double-fire debounce between the in-app QShortcut and the portal Activated paths (500 ms window) so a focused system doesn't hide-then-show the window in one frame.

Added: qtKeySequenceToPortalTrigger helper that converts Qt's "Ctrl+Shift+`" into the freedesktop shortcut-syntax "CTRL+SHIFT+grave". Deliberately minimal — modifiers plus a handful of common punctuation; unrecognised keys pass through.

Invariant: new tests/features/global_shortcuts_portal/ regression test pins (1) isAvailable() gate on portal construction; (2) canonical D-Bus service / path / interface / method literals; (3) bound id "toggle-quake" matches the Activated handler filter; (4) in-app QShortcut fallback stays wired; (5) both activation paths debounce via m_lastQuakeToggleMs; (6) CMakeLists.txt lists the source + keeps Qt6::DBus linked; (7) header surfaces the expected public API.

description.p: Wayland-native Quake-mode, part 1 of 2. When layer-shell-qt6 is present at build time, Ants Terminal's drop-down Quake mode now promotes its window to a zwlr_layer_surface_v1 at the top layer, anchored to the top edge of the active screen — the Wayland equivalent of the X11 _NET_WM_STATE_ABOVE + client-side move() the XCB path has used all along. Also fixes a long-standing dead-code bug: the quake_hotkey config key was saved from Settings but never wired to a shortcut; the QLineEdit value is now honoured in-app. True out-of-focus global hotkey registration (via the Freedesktop Portal GlobalShortcuts API) is the 0.6.39 follow-up.

ul.li: Added: LayerShellQt integration — CMakeLists.txt does find_package(LayerShellQt CONFIG QUIET) and defines ANTS_WAYLAND_LAYER_SHELL when found. Missing = Wayland Quake falls back to the Qt toplevel path; X11 Quake is unaffected.
Fixed: the quake_hotkey config key is now wired to a QShortcut invoking toggleQuakeVisibility(). Pre-0.6.38 the Settings dialog saved the user's chosen sequence but nothing consumed it.

Fixed: the slide-up/slide-down animation no longer snaps on Wayland — QPropertyAnimation on pos() is a no-op there (the compositor owns position), so the Wayland path uses a plain show/hide toggle. XCB slide animation is unchanged.
Packaging: openSUSE spec adds BuildRequires: cmake(LayerShellQt); Arch PKGBUILD adds layer-shell-qt to makedepends + optdepends; Debian control adds liblayershellqt6-dev to Build-Depends.

Invariant: new tests/features/wayland_quake_mode/ regression test pins (1) the layer-shell include is guarded by ANTS_WAYLAND_LAYER_SHELL; (2) setupQuakeMode() dispatches X11 vs Wayland at runtime; (3) toggleQuakeVisibility() skips the pos() animation on Wayland; (4) the quake_hotkey → QShortcut wire exists; (5) CMake uses the QUIET flavour of find_package.

description.p: Phase 3 of the 0.7.0 threading refactor. The ANTS_SINGLE_THREADED=1 kill-switch introduced in 0.6.34 is retired, and the legacy single-threaded read/parse code paths are deleted from TerminalWidget. The bake period (0.6.34 → 0.6.37) proved the worker path; the 0.6.35 hotfix proved the kill-switch worked. Every launch now goes through VtStream on the worker thread — one path, one set of invariants, four feature tests.

ul.li: Removed: the ANTS_SINGLE_THREADED=1 environment kill-switch and the legacy TerminalWidget::startShell() branch it guarded.
Removed: legacy m_pty / m_parser members and the onPtyData slot — the widget no longer owns a Pty or VtParser directly; both live inside VtStream on the worker thread.
Changed: hasPty() and ptyChildPid() simplified to worker-only checks; no behaviour change at the call sites.

description.p: Tenth periodic audit. Two actionable findings across ~110 raw — a 2% signal ratio consistent with the codebase's audit-calibration anchor. Both fixed in a single pass.

ul.li: Fixed: RuleQualityTracker::prune() was dead code — save() inlined the same retention logic. Removed the dead function; save() is now the single source of truth for the 90-day cutoff.
Fixed: pipeRun regex in paste-confirmation was recompiled on every paste. Hoisted to static const so the pattern compiles once per process.

description.p: Hotfix for the 0.6.34 threaded-parse refactor. Keystrokes typed into the terminal were silently dropped — the echo never appeared on screen. Twelve PTY-write call sites in TerminalWidget (keystrokes, arrow keys, paste, focus reporting, scratchpad, context menu, and more) still carried pre-0.6.34 if (m_pty) ptyWrite(...) guards. Under the threaded parse path m_pty is null (the Pty now lives on the worker thread inside VtStream), so the guard silently swallowed every write. Replaced each with either hasPty() (path-agnostic) or an unconditional ptyWrite(...) call (the helper is null-safe). A new threaded_ptywrite_gating feature test pins the invariant at the source level so the bug can't come back quietly.

ul.li: Fixed: keystrokes and paste no longer silently drop on the threaded parse path.
Fixed: shellPid() / shellCwd() / foregroundProcess() now work on the worker path via a new ptyChildPid() helper.
Added: threaded_ptywrite_gating regression test — source-level guard against stale m_pty null-checks outside the allowlisted legacy-path functions.

description.p: Decouples PTY read and VT parsing from the Qt GUI thread. Previously, every byte of terminal output traversed the GUI thread through QSocketNotifier → Pty::dataReceived → VtParser::feed → TerminalGrid::processAction and then on to paint — so heavy streams (find /, cat biglog) blocked scroll and repaint. As of 0.6.34 read + parse run on a dedicated worker QThread (VtStream); only batched VtAction streams cross back to GUI for grid application and paint. Ships the first 0.7.0 performance item early since the architectural change landed clean with three feature-conformance tests locking the invariants.

ul.li: Added: VtStream worker wrapper (src/vtstream.{h,cpp}) — owns Pty and VtParser on a dedicated QThread, accumulates parser output into VtBatch and ships it to GUI over Qt::QueuedConnection. Grid and paint path stay on GUI so the refactor surface stays small.

Added: back-pressure — worker buffers up to 8 batches (~128 KB of unprocessed PTY bytes) before disabling its read notifier so the kernel applies flow control to the child; GUI re-enables reads on drain. Replaces "let RAM grow unbounded while GUI catches up" with kernel-natural flow control.
Added: Pty::setReadEnabled(bool) for the back-pressure path. Pty::write and Pty::resize are now slots so they can be invoked cross-thread via QMetaObject::invokeMethod.

Added: three feature-conformance tests under tests/features/ — threaded_parse_equivalence (11 fixtures × 6 chunking strategies, pins action-stream identity), threaded_response_ordering (DA1/CPR/DA2/mode-996/DSR-OK order across 3 feed strategies), and threaded_resize_synchronous (source-level contract that invokeMethod uses Qt::BlockingQueuedConnection).

Changed: all PTY-write call sites in TerminalWidget route through a new ptyWrite() helper — keystrokes, bracketed paste, Kitty kbd protocol, response callbacks, snippets, scratchpad. The helper branches on worker-active vs. legacy path; correctness is centralised.
Changed: resize path uses Qt::BlockingQueuedConnection so the PTY winsize is updated before resizeEvent returns — no 1-frame flicker at the new grid size with old PTY dims.

Changed: session logging and asciicast timestamps are now sampled on the worker at batch-flush time rather than on GUI at onPtyData time; asciicast recordings are more accurate under paint load. Log write granularity is per-batch rather than per-PTY-read (coarser but still monotonic).

description.p: Kill switch: set ANTS_SINGLE_THREADED=1 in the environment to force the legacy single-threaded path while the new code proves itself. It will be removed in a follow-up release once no regressions surface.

Nine long-standing user-reported bugs around the status bar, scrollback integrity, session restore, and the Review Changes dialog — fixed in one pass. The quiet centerpiece is the root-cause diagnosis of the long tail of "Review Changes button does nothing" reports: a 0.6.26-era focusChanged lambda was firing a singleShot(0) refocus between a button's mousePress and mouseRelease, ripping focus off the button mid-click and silently cancelling the clicked() signal. Also retires the ElidedLabel + stylesheet-chip-padding class of bug by converting every fixed-width status-bar chip to plain QLabel / QPushButton with QSizePolicy::Fixed.

ul.li: Added: one-click "Install Claude Code status-bar hooks" action in Settings > General — writes ~/.config/ants-terminal/hooks/claude-forward.sh and idempotently merges PreToolUse / PostToolUse / Stop / PreCompact / SessionStart entries into ~/.claude/settings.json, preserving any existing hooks.

Added: frozen-view scrollback snapshot — while scrolled up, cellAtGlobal / combiningAt read from a snapshot of the screen at scroll-up time, not the live grid, so the viewport is completely immune to live screen writes. Locked by tests/features/scrollback_frozen_view/.

Added: richer Claude status vocabulary (idle / thinking / bash / reading / editing / searching / browsing / planning / auditing / prompting / compacting) with distinct theme colours; plan-mode and /audit detection in the transcript parser; new planModeChanged and auditingChanged signals; notification_timeout_ms and tab_color_sequence config keys.

Changed: Review Changes button is now tri-state — hidden when not a git repo, disabled when clean and in-sync, enabled otherwise. Probe switched to git status --porcelain=v1 -b so unpushed commits count as reviewable work. The global QPushButton:hover rule is now :enabled-gated. The dialog is fully async — opens instantly with a placeholder and streams git output in, with the status-bar button disabled while it is open.

Changed: status-bar layout uses QSizePolicy::Fixed across the branch / process / Claude-status / button chips. Only the transient notification slot is elastic and elides. Branch chip colour moved from green to cyan so it is visually distinct from Claude-state chips.

Fixed: the focus-redirect race — the focusChanged lambda now exempts QAbstractButton focus targets and defers while a mouse button is held down, so button clicks on status-bar chrome are no longer cancelled mid-press.

Fixed: scrollback blank-line bloat on inactive tabs after session restart. Two root causes: recalcGridSize ran on unlaid-out tabs and shrank grids to 3×10 (pre-layout guard added); TerminalGrid::resize pushed every reflowed row to scrollback (now skips entirely-blank rows).
Fixed: tab colours not persisting across restarts — UUID-keyed tab_groups only worked with session persistence enabled; an ordered tab_color_sequence fallback is now applied at startup.
Fixed: Add-to-Allowlist button gains a 3-scan debounce against TUI-repaint flicker and a shared toolFinished / sessionStopped retraction path across scroll-scan and hook detections.

description.p: Audit self-learning layer + contributor-facing docs + a one-line CI hotfix for 0.6.31's OSC 133 verifier. The headline is a per-rule fire/suppression tracker that records every audit finding and user suppression to <project>/audit_rule_quality.json, plus a new "📊 Rule Quality" button in the audit dialog that surfaces the noisiest rules first. A longest-common-substring suggester turns the last few suppressions for a noisy rule into a one-line dropIfContains proposal that's a copy-paste away from the user's audit_rules.json override pack.

ul.li: Added — audit self-learning: RuleQualityTracker (src/auditrulequality.{h,cpp}) records per-rule fire and suppression events with a 90-day rolling window and 50 000-record-per-category cap. Saves on dialog destruction (RAII) and force-flushes on each suppression so user actions are durable.

Added — Rule Quality dialog: new modal table view listing per-rule 30-day fires, 30-day suppressions, FP rate (capped 0-100 %), all-time fires, and a proposed dropIfContains tightening when the heuristic suggester has enough samples. Sorted by FP rate desc so the noisiest rules surface first.

Added — LCS tightening suggester: longest common substring across the last 5 suppressed line texts per rule. Floor at 6 chars + at least one structural boundary character (space, paren, brace, bracket, semicolon, quote, angle bracket) so we propose rule-shape filters, not project-noun filters. Surfaces inline in the status bar on every suppression.

Added — docs: docs/RECOMMENDED_ROUTINES.md captures the four Claude Code routines that earn a slot on the shared account quota for this repo — nightly audit triage, PR security review, weekly audit-rule self-triage (the deeper LLM-driven counterpart to the always-on tracker), and a monthly ROADMAP-item scoper.

Fixed — CI build: 0.6.31's OSC 133 verifier used a QByteArrayView overload of QMessageAuthenticationCode::addData not available on the Ubuntu runner's Qt 6.x. Switched to the (const char*, qsizetype) overload present everywhere; behaviour unchanged.

description.p: Security + reliability release. The headline is OSC 133 HMAC-signed shell integration: when $ANTS_OSC133_KEY is set, every prompt / command / exit marker must carry a matching HMAC-SHA256 tag, and forged markers from in-terminal processes are dropped (with a throttled status-bar warning). When the key is unset, behaviour is unchanged from 0.6.30 — no upgrade penalty for users who don't opt in.

ul.li: Security: OSC 133 HMAC verification closes the spoofing surface where any process writing to stdout (a malicious TUI, cat malicious.txt) could otherwise mint shell-integration markers and pollute prompt regions, exit codes, and the command_finished plugin event. Shell hooks ship under ${datarootdir}/ants-terminal/shell-integration/ for bash and zsh.

Fixed: Review Changes status-bar button no longer silently swallows clicks on a clean repo — the disabled-but-visible state was advertising itself as actionable via the global hover stylesheet while never emitting clicked(). The button is now hidden on clean repos and reappears the moment a real diff exists.

Fixed: Shift+Enter no longer wedges the terminal tab in bracketed-paste mode (0.6.26 regression). A QByteArray(literal, 8) truncation dropped the closing [201~ end-paste marker; switched to QByteArrayLiteral so the size is derived at compile time.

Fixed: Status-bar Add-to-allowlist button no longer stacks duplicates when a scroll-scan detection fires while a hook-path container is already visible — dedup lookup now uses QWidget* for parity with the other lookup sites.

Fixed — audit signal-to-noise: Two-pass triage of the Ants and Vestige audit runs cuts the false-positive rate from ~97% to under 10% with no loss of real coverage. Tightened memory_patterns, secrets_scan, file_perms, and header_guards rules; dropped clazy qt-keywords; collapsed clang-tidy driver-error storms; added context-window suppressions to six rules; demoted tool timeouts and advisory checks to Info severity.

description.p: Contributor-facing workflow infrastructure release. No runtime or behavior changes for end users — the binary is bit-identical to 0.6.29 modulo the version string. New files are tooling for contributors who clone the repo and work on it through Claude Code.

ul.li: New .claude/settings.json wires a PostToolUse hook on Edit|Write that delegates to packaging/hook-on-cmakelists-edit.sh. The script runs check-version-drift.sh the moment an agent edits CMakeLists.txt and surfaces drift via systemMessage — closes the honor-system gap that allowed the 0.6.23 packaging slip.
New .claude/bump.json recipe consumed by a user-level /bump skill. Lists the 6 version-bearing files with templated find/replace, plus 4 todos for content the recipe can't synthesise.
New packaging/hook-on-cmakelists-edit.sh: small bash dispatcher; stdin is PostToolUse JSON, stdout is empty on clean state or a systemMessage on drift; always exits 0.

.gitignore narrowed .claude/ → .claude/* with explicit exceptions for settings.json and bump.json so the team-shared workflow files commit while developer-local settings.local.json and worktrees/ stay ignored.

description.p: Status-bar reliability pass — nail down every user-reported symptom in one go: git branch chip "…" under tight layout, Review Changes button not appearing until a tab-switch, Add-to-Allowlist button silently no-op on save failure, Claude status label 2 s stale after tab-switch.

ul.li: ElidedLabel::minimumSizeHint now returns the full-text width (capped at maximumWidth). Short text is guaranteed to render without elision regardless of layout squeeze — the statusbar's QBoxLayout previously compressed chips to a 3-char minimum and the text came out as "…" even with ample space. sizeHint also no longer reflects post-elision text (avoiding the chicken-and-egg loop where elision fed its own minimum).

Review Changes button is now refreshed on every 2 s status-bar tick (not only tab-switch + Claude hook fileChanged). Dirty repos on startup show the button immediately; manual git add / git commit / external editor workflows flip the button live without needing Claude hooks.

ClaudeIntegration::setShellPid calls pollClaudeProcess() immediately on PID change so the status label reflects the new tab within one event-loop iteration rather than being NotRunning for up to 2 s until the next timer tick.

ClaudeAllowlistDialog::saveSettings now returns bool; all three callers (prompt-prefill, OK, Apply) surface failure to the user via status message or validation label. Previously every open/write/commit failure was swallowed and the button appeared to do nothing.

Add-to-Allowlist button is now prompt-scoped in both creation paths: the hook-server button-group QWidget has objectName "claudeAllowBtn" matching the scroll-scan path, so onTabChanged cleans up both; the hook-server path's cleared-listener covers every terminal (not just currentTerminal()) so approving on the originating tab after a brief visit to another still clears the button.

showDiffViewer replaces every silent return with an explicit status message so the Review Changes button can't click through to nothing — previously a failed focusedTerminal, empty shellCwd, or non-zero git exit all disappeared without feedback. Combined git diff HEAD in a single invocation (covers worktree + index) rather than two.
New tests/features/status_bar_elision feature test locks the short-text-never-elides invariant inside a real QMainWindow + QStatusBar harness; fails against pre-fix ElidedLabel, passes on fix.

description.p: End-to-end feature review — same-class overflow sweep across the status bar, title bar, and audit dialog; quake-mode toggle fix; combining-character preservation on resize; disk-write durability hardening; auto-profile regex caching.

ul.li: New ElidedLabel widget truncates long text with "…" to a bounded width. Applied to the three status-bar text slots (git branch, transient message, foreground process), the window title label, the Claude status label, and the audit-dialog status label — each previously grew unbounded and pushed siblings off-screen.

Quake-mode toggle no longer auto-hides the window 200 ms after a show toggle. The reused QPropertyAnimation's finished→hide() slot from a prior hide toggle was still connected on the show branch; the slot is now disconnected before every start() and re-attached only on the hide branch.

Terminal resize preserves the per-line combining-character side table on both main and alt screens. Pre-0.6.28 every resize wiped accents, ZWJ sequences, and diacritics because the simple-copy path walked cells only. Shrink cases cleanly evict combining entries whose column falls outside the new width. New tests/features/combining_on_resize feature test pins the three invariants.
Config and session writes now fsync() the temp file before atomic rename, closing the kernel-crash data-loss window on ext4 data=ordered.

Auto-profile rule regex is cached across the 2 s poll tick instead of recompiled per-rule-per-tick, and invalid patterns are detected via QRegularExpression::isValid() and warned once rather than silently never matching.

description.p: Seventh release in the 0.6 UX polish pass — ncurses clear scrollback fix, scroll-offset clamp, tab-close visibility, Claude "prompting" state.

ul.li: ncurses clear now correctly wipes scrollback on TERM=xterm-256color. The 0.6.26 Ink-overflow-repaint heuristic was false-matching the byte-burst because clear's \E[H\E[2J\E[3J sequence overlaps with Ink's clearTerminal. Disambiguated via cursor position at 3J time — clear emits H first so the cursor sits at (0,0); Ink emits H last so the cursor is at the bottom.
Scroll-to-bottom button no longer lingers when scrollback shrinks. m_scrollOffset is now re-clamped in the single updateScrollBar() choke-point after every output batch.
Tab close (×) button is always visible — removed the stylesheet rule that hid the glyph and left only an invisible hover hit-target.

Claude status bar now shows "Claude: prompting" when a permission prompt is waiting, even if the transcript-driven state still reports idle/thinking. Critical when the user is scrolled up in history and can't see the live prompt.

description.p: UX polish pass — status bar consistency, focus handling, Ink overflow-repaint fix, and tab colour gradient.

ul.li: Claude Code (Ink/React TUI) overflow-repaint no longer destroys scrollback or duplicates the replay. CSI 2J followed within 50 ms by CSI 3J on the main screen is now treated as Ink's clearTerminal frame-reset marker (see ink/build/ink.js:705) rather than a user-initiated scrollback wipe — scrollback is preserved and the suppression window is armed for the subsequent replay. Standalone CSI 3J (user-initiated clear -x) still wipes scrollback as expected. Two new feature-conformance scenarios assert both behaviours.

Status bar is now height-stable. QStatusBar's size hint had been tracking its tallest child, so the bar grew when the transient "Add to allowlist" button appeared and shrank when it went away. A 32 px minimum-height floor keeps the bar consistent whether or not a button is attached.

"Review Changes" status-bar button now matches the sibling "Add to allowlist" button's enabled look — it inherits the global QPushButton style instead of its own custom stylesheet (the 0.6.26 "bold to fix Gruvbox contrast" attempt made lowercase letters squish into a small-caps look on Fusion + monospace). Disabled state gets its own visual language (dashed border, italic, muted fg) so a clean-repo state reads clearly as non-actionable.

Git branch chip now has a hard vertical divider separating it from the transient-status slot, visible on low-contrast themes where the chip's rounded-background treatment was indistinguishable from the status bar background.

Focus auto-returns to the active terminal whenever focus lands on chrome widgets (status bar buttons, tab bar) with no dialog open. Clicking a status bar button, answering a prompt, etc., no longer leaves the keyboard parked on the button.

Claude Code context-usage progress bar is hidden until the first real (non-zero) context percentage arrives. A fresh tab, or a tab where Claude was never started, no longer paints a persistent "0%" widget.

Tab-colour badge replaced with a vertical gradient (transparent top → chosen colour bottom). The active-tab 2 px accent underline is preserved by excluding the bottom two pixels from the gradient area.

description.p: User-reported scrollback regression fix plus packaging-rule hardening.

ul.li: Scrolled-up viewport no longer shifts when streaming content arrives. The 0.6.21 pause and 0.6.22/0.6.24 CSI-clear window both skipped scrollback pushes, which froze the scroll anchor and let screen-scroll mechanics bleed into the user's viewport. Fix: push to scrollback while the user is scrolled up (anchor adjusts m_scrollOffset to compensate); the doubling-protection window still fires for the at-bottom case where the doubling symptom is observable.

Extended the packaging-version-drift audit rule to scan org.ants.Terminal.metainfo.xml. The file's <release version=> entry is version-coupled to every release but was not previously checked. Flagged by the ninth periodic audit (2026-04-15).

Extracted the drift-check logic into packaging/check-version-drift.sh — a standalone script that is the single source of truth. Both src/auditdialog.cpp and the CI workflow invoke the same script, so they can no longer drift apart.
Wired the script into .github/workflows/ci.yml as a gating check; a PR that bumps CMakeLists.txt without updating every packaging file now fails CI instead of merging and requiring a follow-up tag.

description.p: Status-bar button-lifecycle and legibility fixes alongside the full-clear scrollback suppression extension.

ul.li: Status-bar "Add to allowlist" button now persists for the full life of the Claude Code permission prompt — previously the button was retracted on the next repaint tick, vanishing within seconds of the prompt appearing.

"Review Changes" button disabled-state text now readable on every theme. Previous textSecondary-based fix still failed WCAG AA on One Dark / Kanagawa; switched to textPrimary + italic + dissolved border so the label stays legible while still communicating the disabled state.

Extended the 0.6.22 suppression window to CSI H; CSI 0J and corner-cursor CSI 1J — both produce the same post-state as CSI 2J and therefore exhibit the same doubling bug on TUI redraw bursts. Regression tests for both shapes added to tests/features/scrollback_redraw/.

Packaging recipes (openSUSE spec, Arch PKGBUILD, Debian changelog, man page) bumped to 0.6.24 after drifting two releases behind — the exact drift the packaging-version-drift audit rule was written to catch.

description.p: Three user-reported UX fixes, an event-driven status-bar contract, and the first 0.7.0 performance item — SIMD VT-parser scan.

ul.li: Allowlist "already covered" check no longer blocks legitimate compound rules — subsumption now splits on shell operators (&&, ||, ;, |) and requires every segment to match before declaring coverage.
Right-click tab colour picker now renders visibly — a new ColoredTabBar paints a 3-px strip outside stylesheet influence (was defeated by QSS specificity).
Status bar clears event-scoped transients (Allow buttons, notifications) on tab switch so state from one tab never leaks into another.
Fixed a Shift+Enter paste-image regression.
SIMD ground-state scan (SSE2 / NEON) — bulk Print emission on runs of safe printable-ASCII, with byte-identical action-stream regression guards.

description.p: Root-cause fix for main-screen TUI scrollback doubling, plus packaging catch-up across all recipes.

ul.li: Scrollback no longer accumulates duplicates when a main-screen TUI (Claude Code v2.1+) repaints via CSI 2J — a 250 ms sliding window now suppresses scrollback pushes during the repaint burst.
Packaging recipes (openSUSE spec, Arch PKGBUILD, Debian changelog) bumped to 0.6.22 after drifting three releases behind.
openSUSE spec gains %post/%postun scriptlets for desktop-database and icon-cache refresh.
Debian gains postinst/postrm scripts for the same purpose.

description.p: Audit-tool noise reduction, dialog theming, and a scrollback-insert pause for users actively reading history while a TUI repaints.

ul.li: Fixed a GNU grep 3.12 argument-ordering quirk that silently disabled the audit's --include file-type filter.
Extended the QSS cascade so every popup dialog inherits the terminal's active theme.
Added a scrollback-insert pause that stops TUI redraws from interleaving intermediate frames into the user's reading position.

description.p: Distribution-adoption bundle H5: OBS, AUR, and Debian packaging recipes.
Distribution-readiness bundle H4: bash, zsh, and fish shell completions plus CMake install rules.
Distribution-readiness bundle H3: ants-terminal(1) man page with CMake install rule.
Distribution-readiness bundle H2: AppStream metainfo and spec-compliant desktop entry.

ul.li: Added org.ants.Terminal.metainfo.xml for GNOME Software and KDE Discover discovery.
Added org.ants.Terminal.desktop following the reverse-DNS naming convention.
CMake install rules for desktop entry, metainfo, and hicolor-theme icons at six sizes.
CI validates packaging artefacts with appstreamcli and desktop-file-validate.

description.p: Distribution-readiness bundle H1: community and security policy documents.

ul.li: Added CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (Contributor Covenant 2.1) with dedicated reporting channels.
Changelogged the previously shipped SECURITY.md coordinated-disclosure policy.

description.p: Incremental scrollback reflow with a fast path for standalone lines.
Security hardening — URI scheme allowlist in addRowHyperlink.
